phases.name: Discovery + model list source
phases.goal: Confirm current Groq free models and map existing Productivity provider architecture

todos.text: Audit current Productivity provider/model flow in ProductivtyChat, SettingsModal, productivity/agent
Verify Groq API compatibility path (OpenAI-compatible chat/completions) and auth header usage
Collect an initial list of Groq free model IDs from official docs and define refresh strategy

phases.name: Provider + persistence plumbing
phases.goal: Add persistent Groq API key + provider preference

todos.text: Add provider selection persistence (codex | groq) in localStorage
Add secure-ish storage approach for Groq API key (localStorage for now unless you want encrypted main-process storage)
Implement helper utilities for reading/writing provider settings and key

phases.name: UI/UX updates
phases.goal: Expose Groq in Settings and chat model picker with key-required UX

todos.text: Add Groq provider card in Settings → Productivity with connect/disconnect states
Add Productivity chat provider/model picker entries for Groq
If provider is Groq and key is missing, show modal prompt to enter key before sending
Allow entering/changing key from modal and from Settings

phases.name: Agent runtime integration
phases.goal: Route requests to Codex or Groq based on selected provider/model

todos.text: Refactor runProductivityAgent to support provider-specific transport
Keep existing Codex auth flow unchanged
Add Groq request path with Bearer API key and compatible payload mapping
Normalize streaming/non-streaming response handling into existing message pipeline

phases.name: Validation + release notes
phases.goal: Ensure flow works end-to-end and document changes

todos.text: Manual smoke: Codex unchanged; Groq works with key; missing key modal appears
Commit and push after your review

planTitle: Add Groq provider with API-key flow and model selection
